n. f. S. कुक्षि Pr. kuchchhī , Pālī, kuchchhī . 1. The belly; hypochondrium . 2. The womb. 1. Yā to kachhū kokh meṅ kaj merī hai, Yā tum meṅ kachhu kaj haigī, balmā, Terī javānī meṅ bāṅjh rahi, re balmā! Farrukh. Wom. song. Or in my womb's a twist not right, Or you, my spouse, are not fit quite, I'm barren, spouse, your youth despite! 2. Kokh paṛte hī na mar gayā? A curse. Why didn't you die when you conceived? kokh-band, kokh-jalī , n. f. A barren woman. kokh-pīrā, kokh kā āzār . Disease of the womb. kokh shāstar . See ilm-i-gaib. Maiṅ kokh shāstar nahīṅ paṛhī hūṅ . Wom. kokh kī āṅch yā māmṭā , n. f. Maternal love. kokh mārī jānā ; Tir. koṅkhi māral gel , v. n. To become barren. kokheṅ lag jānā , v. n. To sink (the belly).

def: n. m. An oil press; a sugar mill. Til hue kolhū pile, baiṭh pilāyā aṅg, Ham to ghar ghar jalte phire, tū sun be yār pataṅg! As sesame seed I was crushed in the mill, I sat and gave my body to be bruised. Now I burn, borne about from house to house: listen, O lover, moth! kolhū chalnā, yā khaṛe h. , v. n. To set up a mill. kolhū kā bail , n. m. See telī kā bail under tel. kolkū kāṭ mogrā banānā , v. a. To make a mallet out of a mill; to destroy a cannon to make a needle. kolhū ke bail kī tarah pilnā , v. n. 1. To work as oxen in an oilmill. 2. To work as a galley slave; to work very hard. kolhū meṅ pilvā denā , v. a. To crush in a mill. zan bachcha kolhū meṅ pilvānā . A native punishment of crushing a whole family in a mill.
s.m. A caste of Hindūs who are weavers; a man of that caste, a weaver.
more: n. m. S. कौलिक A weaver ( julāhā , 1).

def: adj. (f. -ī), 'Virgin,' just as made, untouched, unhandled, unused, new, fresh, unbleached, unwashed; plain, undyed (cloth or silk); unwritten, clean, blank (paper); unoccupied (house, &c.); unhackneyed; unversed, unpractised; unprofited, unimproved, with nothing gained or obtained; untutored, simple, stupid; — unsubstantial; unprofitable, fruitless, empty; bare, mere, simple; — poor, penniless, destitute; — s.m. Unbleached cloth; plain or undyed silk, 'corah'; — an ignorant fellow, a simpleton, a fool
more: adj. S. केवल 1. Blank ( sādā , 1). 2. New; unused. 3. Unbleached (cloth, etc.). 4. Untutored; untaught. Chār baras maktab meṅ paṛhe, par kore hī rahe . 5. Simple; stupid. Tum bhī kore chālīs sere ho! Prov. Well! you are a fool full standard weight! 6. ( koram-kār ) Poor; penniless; destitute. korā , adv. Only (خالي 1). Ek billī, ek baglā dekhā, oṛh faqīrī khilkā, Bāhar se sab gyān ko chhāṅṭeṅ, andar korā chhilkā, Koī safā na dekhā dil kā! Bhajan. Kabīr. In friar's robes a cat and eke a crane I saw, without they seemed all truth to know, within mere straw: none pure in heart I saw! korā bachnā , v. n. To escape unhurt; come off with a whole skin. korā bartan , n. 1. A new vessel. 2. ( Met .) A maid; virgin. Tāzgī jī kī aur tarī tan kī, Vāh! kyā bāt kore bartan kī! Double entendre. Nazīr. The frame refreshed, the heart with new life fed, Such zest in vessels new unlimited! korā-pan , n. m.…
n. m. 1. Sweepings; rubbish (آخور). 2. Share; lot. kūṛ'ī, kuṛṛī, kur'aṛ , W.; ginaurā , E. n. f. 1. A dunghill. 2. Dust, ash, or dungheaps. 3. Heaps of refuse for manure.

s.m. 'An undefiled body'; an unmarried person; a bachelor; a virgin or spinster; — maidenhood, virginity

def: s f. Cost of digging out, &c.
n. f. The cost of digging.
s.m. A bud, blossom, an unblown flower.
s.m. The sacred book of the Muḥammadans, the Qorʼān (or Korān, Coran, or Alcoran)
more: n. m. قرا reading. The sacred book of the Mahomedans. It consists of revelations made to Mahomed, and delivered by him orally, and collected and committed to writing by the Khalif Omar. 1. Panḍit pothī bāṅchte, mullāṅ paṛhe qurān, Log dikhāvā lākh paḍho, nānh mile Bhagvān . The Pandit Shāstars reads, the Mulla his Quran for show, But let them spout on e'er so much their God is not found so. 2. Qurān par qurān rakhne kā kyā muzāëqā . Prov. Pile on Quran upon Quran, what matters it? (No other thing should be placed on the sacred book). qurān uṭhānā, yā sir par rakhnā, qurān kī qasam khānā, qurān par hāth rakhnā, yā dharnā , v. a. To take an oath on the Qurān ; to kiss the book. Phr. So help me God. Jis bāt kī chāho qasam ek martaba le lo! Har bār to Qurān uṭhāyā nahīṅ jātā . Rind. Once as you will upon the sacred book I'll swear, But constantly the book take up I cannot dare. qurān ṭhanḍā …
subs: To raise the Qorʼān (to swear), to take an oath on the Qorʼān
To place the hand on the Qorʼān (to swear by), to swear on the Qorʼān
To let the Qorʼān fall or drop (he who drops the Qorʼān must give its weight of corn as alms to the poor)
s.m. A reader of the Qorʼān; — a person deposed from rule or dignity, one who is dismissed from office, &c.
'To place the Qorʼān on the head,' to swear on the Qorʼān
'To wear a garment made of the leaves of the Qorʼān'; to become the Qorʼān itself; — to swear excessively; to take an oath on the most trivial occasion
To go or read through the Qorʼān; to repeat the Qorʼān (to one another, two or more people).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قرآن (qur'ān), from Arabic اَلْقُرْآن (al-qurʔān). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi कुरांन (kurāṃna /⁠kurā̃n⁠/) / قرآن (qrān /⁠qurān⁠/).

An adverb. kriyā bigāṛnā, yā kharāb k. , v. a. To defame or disgrace. kriyā-bhaṅg ; A. halaf-darogī , n. Perjury. kriyā bhaṅg k . To disturb or render nugatory a religious work. kriyā karm , n. 1. Religious service; daily observances, such as oblations, prayer, etc. 2. Funeral ceremonies; obsequies. kriyā k. , v. a. To perform one's obsequies. kriyā khānā , v. a. To take an oath.
s. f. 1. Deed, an act, action. 2. A verb. 3. An oath. 4. Obsequies. کرا کرم kriyā-karm, Performance of obsequies.
subs: s.m. Destroying or rendering nugatory a religious act; false swearing, perjury
or kriyā bigāṛnā, To disgrace, defame
vulg. kiriyā-karam, s.m. Religious service or duty, daily observances (such as oblations, prayer, &c.); funeral ceremonies, the performance of obsequies
(or kiriyā) khānā (-kī), To take an oath; to swear (by)
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Engaging in actions, practical; engaged in business; performing religious or pious works; — one who engages in actions, &c.
s.m. (in Gram.) 'That which defines an action more closely,' an adverb
(˚yā+in˚), s.m. An organ of action ( = karmendriya, q.v.s.v. karm).
wety: From Sanskrit क्रिया (kriyā).

def: s.m. Breast, chest, bosom; the lap; the interior (of anything), cavity (cf. kol; kaulā)
subs: s.m. A marginal writing (omitted in the proper place); postscript (to a letter); a supplement.

def: n. m. S. क्रुध to become angry; Pālī, kodho . Rage ( tezī , 6). krodhī, kirodhī , adj. Irascible ( tez , 6).

v.n. To become angry, to get into a rage or passion
or krodh-vant, adj. (f. -vatī) = krodhan, and krodhī, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Free from wrath, mild, gentle; — a gentle person, &c.
vulg. kirodh-bas, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Overpowered by anger, passionate, violent; — one who is under the influence or the sway of wrath.

def: s.m. The being angry, anger; — adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Inclined to wrath, angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric; — a passionate man, &c.
s.f. A passionate woman, a vixen, a scold.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Angry, wrathful, irascible, passionate, choleric; — a passionate or an irascible man.
more: n. m. kirodhan , n. f. A passionate person.

def: adj. = krodhī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Angry, wrathful, irascible, passionate, choleric; — a passionate or an irascible man.
part. adj. Irritated, angry, wrathful.
adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, cholerick.

s.m. = S کرورتا क्रूरता krūratā, s.f. Harshness; cruelty, fierceness; formidableness, &c. (see krūr).

adj. & s.m. Patient, enduring, suffering, submissive; endurable, tolerable; competent, capable, able, fit for; useful, suitable; friendly, favourable, benevolent; — a patient person, &c.; — propriety, fitness.
s.f. Patience, forbearance, indulgence; forgiveness, pardon, absolution, dispensation; — the earth; night ( = kshapā)
subs: adj. Devoid of patience, &c.; impatient; unforgiving
s.m. 'The ocean of forgiveness or forbearance,' an epithet of the Deity
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of a patient or a forgiving disposition; — a person of a forgiving disposition, &c.
(with acc. -ko), To pardon, forgive, excuse; (with abl. -se) to practise forbearance (towards), to bear (with), to forbear, abstain, withhold
or kshamā-vant (f. -vatī) = kshamā-yukt, vulg. kshimā-jukt (f. -ā), adj. Endowed with patience, patient, enduring, forbearing

wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit क्षमा (kṣamā).

part. (f. -ā), Forgiven, pardoned.

ety: ksham˚ = S. क्षमय(ति), caus. of rt. क्षम्
def: v.t. To bear patiently, to endure; to pardon, forgive ( = kshamā karnā, q.v.).
s.m. = kshaṇ, q.v.

s.m. An instant, the twinkling of an eye, a moment; a measure of time equal to thirty kalās or four minutes; a leisure moment, leisure, vacation from work; fit or suitable moment, opportunity; — a festival; the time of new or full moon; — the centre, middle; — adv. For a moment
subs: (˚ṇa+an˚), adv. A momemt afterwards, the next moment, thereupon; moment by moment
adj. In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye
s.f. lit. 'Giving leisure'; night; turmeric; — kshaṇa-dā-ćar, s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), 'Night-walker'; a rākshas, fiend, goblin
adv. Moment by moment, from moment to moment, every moment, continually, constantly, without intermission
adv. Momentarily; merely for a moment
s.m. & adv. One moment, an instant, a little while; — in a moment.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

def: adj. Being at leisure, having a leisure moment; — momentary, transient, &c. ( = kshaṇik).
adj. Momentary, transient, transitory, temporary, fleeting.
